expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves banding requirements, style constraints, and rendering assumptions before scenario drafting
expectations.evidence: The run output explicitly states the opposite: “Phase 1 is limited to generating spawn requests… The benchmark focus requires that the context-intake step explicitly preserve key domain constraints… With only the provided evidence, Phase 1 does not define an explicit mechanism/artifact to capture and preserve those constraints at intake time.” It also notes: “None of the Phase 1 contract language explicitly states that Phase 1 must: extract/record banding functional requirements, capture style constraints…, or lock rendering assumptions… into a dedicated ‘context-intake’ artifact used downstream.”

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: The result.md is explicitly framed as a Phase 1 contract check (“Primary phase under test: phase1”) and discusses Phase 1 contract outputs/validation (spawn manifest, evidence routing). It concludes: “Phase 1 alignment is present (it is Phase 1 content)…”
